api/obj/api/PlcComm.KvHostLink.KvHostLin.title: Class KvHostLinkClientExtensions | KEYENCE Host Link .NET Documentation
api/obj/api/PlcComm.KvHostLink.KvHostLin.keywords: Class KvHostLinkClientExtensions Namespace PlcComm.KvHostLink Assembly PlcComm.KvHostLink.dll High-level helper API for KvHostLinkClient. public static class KvHostLinkClientExtensions Inheritance object KvHostLinkClientExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks These extension methods are the recommended user-facing surface for normal application code. They wrap the token-oriented low-level client API with typed reads and writes, bit-in-word helpers, named snapshots, polling, and one-step connection setup. Methods OpenAndConnectAsync(string, int, CancellationToken) Creates a KvHostLinkClient and opens the connection. public static Task<KvHostLinkClient> OpenAndConnectAsync(string host, int port = 8501, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters host string PLC IP address or hostname. port int KV HostLink TCP port. Defaults to 8501. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<KvHostLinkClient> A connected KvHostLinkClient. Remarks This is the recommended entry point for samples and application code that want a ready-to-use client in one step. Dispose the returned client when the connection is no longer needed. PollAsync(KvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, TimeSpan, CancellationToken) Continuously polls the specified addresses and yields a snapshot each cycle. public static IAsyncEnumerable<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> PollAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, IEnumerable<string> addresses, TimeSpan interval, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. addresses IEnumerable<string> Address strings in the same format as ReadNamedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, CancellationToken). interval TimeSpan Time between polls. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token to stop polling. Returns IAsyncEnumerable<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> Remarks If the address set is batchable, the compiled read plan is reused on every iteration for lower per-cycle overhead. ReadDWordsAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values starting at device. public static Task<uint[]> ReadDWordsAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Starting device address (for example "DM0"). count int Number of 32-bit values to read. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<uint[]> Unsigned 32-bit values combined from adjacent word pairs. Remarks Adjacent words are combined in low-word, high-word order. This matches the helper-layer interpretation used by ReadTypedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, string, CancellationToken) for "D" and "F". ReadNamedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, IEnumerable<string>, CancellationToken) Reads multiple named values and returns a snapshot dictionary. public static Task<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> ReadNamedAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, IEnumerable<string> addresses, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. addresses IEnumerable<string> Address strings that specify both the base device and the desired interpretation. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<IReadOnlyDictionary<string, object>> A dictionary keyed by the original input address strings. Remarks Address format examples: "DM100" -- unsigned 16-bit (ushort) "DM100:F" -- float "DM100:S" -- signed 16-bit (short) "DM100:D" -- unsigned 32-bit "DM100:L" -- signed 32-bit "DM100.3" -- bit 3 within word (bool) "DM100.A" -- bit 10 within word (bool); bits 10-15 use hex digits A-F Bit-in-word indices use hexadecimal notation (0-F), matching the KEYENCE address format. Bits 0-9 can be written as decimal digits; bits 10-15 must be written as A-F. For example, bit 12 is addressed as "DM100.C", not "DM100.12". When all requested addresses are compatible with helper-layer batching, this method merges contiguous reads into one or more RDS operations. Mixed or non-optimizable address sets fall back to sequential helper reads with the same return shape. ReadTypedAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, string, CancellationToken) Reads a single device value and converts it to a high-level CLR type. public static Task<object> ReadTypedAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, string dtype, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Base device address string, for example "DM100". dtype string High-level data type code: "U" = ushort, "S" = short, "D" = uint, "L" = signed 32-bit int, "F" = IEEE 754 float32. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<object> A boxed CLR value. Integer formats return boxed integral types and "F" returns a boxed float. Remarks The float helper is implemented at the extension layer by reading two consecutive .U words and combining them as low-word, high-word. ReadWordsAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, CancellationToken) Reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit words starting at device. public static Task<ushort[]> ReadWordsAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int count, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Starting device address (e.g. "DM0"). count int Number of words to read. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task<ushort[]> Unsigned word values in PLC order. Remarks This helper is the preferred user-facing block-read API for contiguous word devices. WriteBitInWordAsync(KvHostLinkClient, string, int, bool, CancellationToken) Performs a read-modify-write to set or clear a single bit inside a word device. public static Task WriteBitInWordAsync(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, int bitIndex, bool value, CancellationToken ct = default) Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Base word device address string, for example "DM100". bitIndex int Bit position within the word (0–15). value bool New bit value. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task Remarks This helper operates on word-oriented devices such as DM. It is distinct from PLC force-set / force-reset commands for bit device families. Exceptions ArgumentOutOfRangeException Thrown when bitIndex is outside the range 0 to 15. WriteTypedAsync<T>(KvHostLinkClient, string, string, T, CancellationToken) Writes a single device value using a high-level data type code. public static Task WriteTypedAsync<T>(this KvHostLinkClient client, string device, string dtype, T value, CancellationToken ct = default) where T : IFormattable Parameters client KvHostLinkClient The client to use. device string Base device address string, for example "DM100". dtype string High-level data type code: "U", "S", "D", "L", or "F". value T Value to write. ct CancellationToken Cancellation token. Returns Task Type Parameters T Remarks The float helper is implemented at the extension layer by converting the input value to IEEE 754 float32 and writing two consecutive .U words.

user/DEVICE_HANDLING.html.title: Device and Type Handling | KEYENCE Host Link .NET Documentation
user/DEVICE_HANDLING.html.keywords: Device and Type Handling This page describes device handling from the recommended high-level API. Typed Helpers ReadTypedAsync and WriteTypedAsync are the standard entry points for single typed values. dtype Meaning Width .NET type U unsigned 16-bit 1 word ushort S signed 16-bit 1 word short D unsigned 32-bit 2 words uint L signed 32-bit 2 words int F IEEE 754 float32 2 words float Example: ushort dm0 = (ushort)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM0", "U"); int counter = (int)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM10", "L"); float temp = (float)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM20", "F"); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM100", "U", dm0); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM110", "L", counter); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM120", "F", temp); Contiguous Areas Use block helpers when the data occupies consecutive word addresses. ushort[] words = await client.ReadWordsAsync("DM200", 8); uint[] dwords = await client.ReadDWordsAsync("DM300", 4); ReadWordsAsync reads contiguous unsigned 16-bit values ReadDWordsAsync reads contiguous unsigned 32-bit values from adjacent words Bit-in-Word Addresses Use WriteBitInWordAsync to update a single bit inside a word device without disturbing the other bits. await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 0, value: true); await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 10, value: false); Mixed Snapshots Use ReadNamedAsync when one application snapshot mixes typed word values and bit-in-word values. Supported notation: Format Meaning "DM100" unsigned 16-bit "DM100:S" signed 16-bit "DM100:D" unsigned 32-bit "DM100:L" signed 32-bit "DM100:F" float32 "DM100.3" bit 3 inside the word "DM100.A" bit 10 inside the word Example: var snapshot = await client.ReadNamedAsync( new[] { "DM100", "DM101:S", "DM102:D", "DM104:F", "DM200.3", "DM200.A" }); Bit indices use hexadecimal notation from 0 to F. Scope This user-facing page intentionally stops at the helper layer. Token-oriented reads, raw suffix handling, and direct protocol operations are documented for maintainers only.

user/USER_GUIDE.html.title: User Guide: Host Link Communication .NET | KEYENCE Host Link .NET Documentation
user/USER_GUIDE.html.keywords: User Guide: Host Link Communication .NET This guide covers the recommended high-level API only. Use these entry points in normal application code: OpenAndConnectAsync ReadTypedAsync WriteTypedAsync WriteBitInWordAsync ReadNamedAsync PollAsync ReadWordsAsync ReadDWordsAsync Raw token-oriented reads, writes, and protocol details are intentionally left to the maintainer documentation. Installation Add a project reference to: src/PlcComm.KvHostLink/PlcComm.KvHostLink.csproj or reference the compiled assembly from a .NET 9 application. Connect Once using PlcComm.KvHostLink; await using var client = await KvHostLinkClientExtensions.OpenAndConnectAsync( "192.168.250.100", 8501); OpenAndConnectAsync returns an open KvHostLinkClient ready for the helper extension methods below. Typed Read and Write Supported dtype codes: dtype Meaning Width U unsigned 16-bit 1 word S signed 16-bit 1 word D unsigned 32-bit 2 words L signed 32-bit 2 words F IEEE 754 float32 2 words ushort u = (ushort)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM0", "U"); short s = (short)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM1", "S"); uint d = (uint)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM2", "D"); float f = (float)await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM4", "F"); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM10", "U", u); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM11", "S", s); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM12", "D", d); await client.WriteTypedAsync("DM14", "F", f); F is implemented in the helper layer by converting two .U words as float32. Block Reads Use block helpers for contiguous data in a word area. ushort[] words = await client.ReadWordsAsync("DM100", 8); uint[] dwords = await client.ReadDWordsAsync("DM200", 4); Bit in Word Use WriteBitInWordAsync for bit updates inside word devices such as DM, EM, FM, W, or Z. await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 0, value: true); await client.WriteBitInWordAsync("DM500", bitIndex: 3, value: false); This helper performs a read-modify-write so that the other 15 bits remain unchanged. Mixed Snapshots with ReadNamedAsync Supported address notation: Format Meaning "DM100" unsigned 16-bit "DM100:S" signed 16-bit "DM100:D" unsigned 32-bit "DM100:L" signed 32-bit "DM100:F" float32 "DM100.3" bit 3 inside the word "DM100.A" bit 10 inside the word var snapshot = await client.ReadNamedAsync( new[] { "DM100", "DM101:S", "DM102:D", "DM104:F", "DM200.0", "DM200.A" }); Bit indices use hexadecimal notation from 0 to F. Polling PollAsync repeatedly yields the same kind of snapshot dictionary. using var cts = new CancellationTokenSource(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10)); await foreach (var snapshot in client.PollAsync( new[] { "DM100", "DM101:L", "DM200.3" }, TimeSpan.FromSeconds(1), cts.Token)) { Console.WriteLine(snapshot["DM100"]); } Error Handling Exception Meaning HostLinkError PLC returned an error code HostLinkProtocolError Local validation or parsing failure HostLinkConnectionError Connect, disconnect, socket, or timeout failure try { var value = await client.ReadTypedAsync("DM100", "U"); } catch (HostLinkProtocolError ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Protocol error: {ex.Message}"); } catch (HostLinkConnectionError ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Connection error: {ex.Message}"); } catch (HostLinkError ex) { Console.WriteLine($"PLC error: {ex.Message}"); } Recommended Samples API / workflow Sample Purpose OpenAndConnectAsync, ReadTypedAsync, WriteTypedAsync, ReadWordsAsync, ReadDWordsAsync, WriteBitInWordAsync, ReadNamedAsync, PollAsync samples/PlcComm.KvHostLink.HighLevelSample/PlcComm.KvHostLink.HighLevelSample.csproj Full helper-layer walkthrough ReadTypedAsync, WriteTypedAsync, ReadWordsAsync, ReadDWordsAsync samples/PlcComm.KvHostLink.BasicReadWriteSample/PlcComm.KvHostLink.BasicReadWriteSample.csproj Focused typed and contiguous block example ReadNamedAsync, WriteBitInWordAsync, PollAsync samples/PlcComm.KvHostLink.NamedPollingSample/PlcComm.KvHostLink.NamedPollingSample.csproj Mixed snapshot and polling example
